title: [Bug]: Backlog sync accepts duplicate JSON keys
summary: The local backlog-to-GitHub sync tool parses backlog item JSON with default json.loads behavior, which silently keeps the last value when duplicate keys are present.
observed: A backlog item with duplicate title or metadata keys can be accepted with only the final value preserved.
expected: Duplicate keys in backlog JSON must be rejected as ambiguous before an issue body can be generated.
reproduction: Run the focused regression test that writes a valid backlog item with a duplicate title key and calls load_backlog_item.
impact: Backlog files become public GitHub issues. Ambiguous JSON could make local review differ from the final published issue and weaken the audit trail.
delivery_semantics: No runtime delivery behavior changes. This fix hardens project-management automation only.
security: The fix must preserve existing public-issue leak prevention and must not publish secrets, URLs, IP addresses, or private environment details.
acceptance: A focused regression test proves duplicate backlog JSON keys are rejected.
Valid backlog JSON files continue to sync.
The rejection message is clear for maintainers.
Existing backlog sync tests and full checks still pass.
tests: Run the focused regression test, backlog sync tests, and the full local check script.
documentation: Update CHANGELOG.md and backlog workflow documentation if public behavior changes.
closeout: Close only after the release containing the fix is published. Include the release tag, regression evidence, verification evidence, and any remaining limitations.
